extraction.one_line_summary: QUINTANA Nairo won from the breakaway, escaping on the Galibier while ALAPHILIPPE Julian defended yellow despite GC teams failing to dislodge him on the roughest Alpine stage
tactical_patterns: QUINTANA joining early breakaway forced Movistar to chase their own rider, limiting final gap that could've threatened GC.
BERNAL Egan attacked final kilometer climb but only gained 27 seconds on THOMAS, revealing Ineos internal hierarchy shift underway.
GC contenders unable to drop ALAPHILIPPE on consecutive Alpine cols suggests sprint-type riders can defend yellow through Stage 18.
BENNETT George crashed at 6km under high speed on technical turn, exposing Jumbo-Visma's vulnerability to late-stage descending incidents.

rider_signals.QUINTANA Nairo: positive: First Tour stage win since 2015, specifically accelerated away from breakaway companions on Galibier to win by 1:35
rider_signals.BERNAL Egan: positive: Attacked teammates THOMAS in final kilometer to move above him in GC, signaling Ineos captaincy transfer
rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: positive: Survived roughest Alpine day only 27 seconds down to winner, making French victory increasingly probable with two climbs remaining
rider_signals.BARDET Romain: neutral: Won KOM jersey back but showed disappointment unable to match Quintana's Galibier acceleration despite second place
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: negative: Lost GC position to BERNAL in final kilometer, indicating defending champion no longer Ineos protected rider
rider_signals.BENNETT George: negative: Heavy crash at 6km on high-speed technical turn required medical attention, second crash of his Tour
